original_file: Which Remote Leaders Have Evolved Beyond Human Intelligence?
email_subject: Which Remote Leaders Have Evolved Beyond Human Intelligence?
email_body:

By Crossover

https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/which-remote-leaders-have-evolved-beyond-human-intelligence-x1qyf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BWIguBjBER%2B%2Bw5FLzsnKRmw%3D%3D&midToken=AQEbAgAs2lKPUw&midSig=33fmLRNoxE9s81&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-lczh4v~ml5c0m5z~1a-null-null&eid=lczh4v-ml5c0m5z-1a

  

The AI meteor already hit - and most people are still staring at the sky. AI’s reshaped the planet, remote work has redefined the terrain, and yesterday’s leadership rules are fossils. This edition celebrates the remote leaders who didn’t just survive the impact - they evolved fast enough to thrive in what came after. 🦖 The Evolutionary Intelligence Edition By Andrew Allen and Carla Dewing ⚡ Top Remote Leaders Don’t Play It Safe - They Evolve The asteroid didn’t send a calendar invite. It just ended the meeting. AI isn’t coming folks - it hit. And remote work is the ground beneath your feet that keeps changing from one day to the next. You either evolve…or die. Extinction doesn’t come with a warning, just a new reality. Like the one you’re in right now. If you’re not rocketing to the top – prepare for the fallout. This year’s Remote Work Leadership Award winners aren’t adapting to AI - they’re thriving in the new atmosphere of work. Here’s what they’ve figured out before the rest of the world even saw the impact crater: ☑️ Remote work rewards evolutionary thinkers, not corporate compliance. Top leaders aren’t micromanaging - they’re designing systems where people self-direct, over-communicate, and build insane outcomes that scale. Why wait for permission to do great things with technology? ☑️ AI isn’t a tool or a mirror. It’s a new layer of intelligence. These winners don’t just use AI - they think with it. They’ve replaced tasks with systems, meetings with metrics, and fragile workflows with robust automation. Their thinking compounds because it’s encoded. Is AI part of your DNA yet? ☑️ Leadership is no longer a title. It’s a distributed skill. Command-and-control is prehistoric. Today’s leadership is about pattern recognition, permissionless decision-making, and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. You don’t need authority to lead - you need evolutionary intelligence. Do you have it? ✅ Do This Now Pick one ancient ritual (meeting, habit, approval loop) and obliterate it. Audit your circle - do they challenge you to evolve or keep you comfy in extinction? Ask: Am I building systems that scale trust… or just ones that need babysitting? The winners have already answered that question. And they’re not waiting for permission to evolve. 👉 Meet the 2025 Remote Work Leadership Award Winners 📰 Breaking News ⚡ A Quick Update on Remote & AI Remote work becomes ultra exclusive, AI skills are the real employment currency, and Big Tech just hard-coded that advantage into the education system. The future of work isn’t arriving… it’s filtering people out, already. Remote work isn’t dying - it’s gated. Only ~6% of jobs are fully remote, which means flexibility is now an elite advantage. Davos says jobs aren’t safe - skills are. AI is splitting workers into evolving vs replaceable, and good vibes won’t save you. Big Tech owns the classroom. Google, Microsoft, and Anthropic are teaching AI early - long before employers ask why YOU didn’t. 🛠️ Tool Share ⚡ Practical Remote Leadership Advice How to Build a Career in AI Without Starting from Scratch. A reality check for leaders who think AI careers mean quitting their job, learning Python, or becoming insufferable on LinkedIn. 7 Essential Thinking Skills for Remote Jobs in 2026. The cognitive upgrades remote leaders actually need now that 'being online' stopped counting as value. How to Become a Lifelong Learner Without Burnout or Bullshit. A smarter way to keep learning without turning your evenings into unpaid self-improvement labor. 5 Characteristics of a Growth Mindset You Can Learn Now. Proof that growth isn’t a personality trait - it’s a set of behaviors most leaders still avoid. 💻 Top Jobs ⚡ Get Hired on Crossover 🌏 Global Remote Roles: Director of Academics, 2 Hour Learning - $400,000 USD/year AI-First VP of Professional Services, IgniteTech - $200,000 USD/year Senior DevOps Engineer, Trilogy - $100,000 USD/year 🗽 US-Based EdTech Roles: SVP of Site Sourcing, 2 Hour Learning - $400,000 USD/year Campus Director, Alpha - $200,000 USD/year Enrolment Systems & Operations Manager, Alpha - $100,000 USD/year 💡 Ask Crossover ⚡ Expert Voices We asked: “What’s the first leadership responsibility people quietly hand over to AI and why is that a mistake?” Ricardo Fernandes, Senior AI Innovation Specialist at IgniteTech answered: "I think leaders quietly started letting AI decide how to say too many important things. Feedback, announcements, bad news. It feels harmless, like you're just delegating the writing. But tone is leadership. The way you deliver a message matters as much as the message itself! Giving feedback to someone who is struggling or announcing bad news requires you to know your audience, feel the weight of the words, and own how they land. And people notice. Maybe not consciously, but they feel when words aren't really yours or directed at them. Leadership requires authenticity and connection, something AI still lacks." 💡 Have a question for a Crossover top performer? Drop it here! 😂 Punchline ⚡ A Laugh Before You Go Andrew Allen is the VP of Content Marketing at Crossover, based in Auckland, New Zealand. He’s worked remotely since 2021, using humor and storytelling to make complex ideas stick. He hosts the world’s most-watched remote work vlog (Out of Office), co-creates one of LinkedIn’s top newsletters (Radically Remote), and contributes to Forbes. Outside work, he’s visited 100+ countries, seen all seven wonders - and his dogs occasionally steal the spotlight. Radically Remote is LinkedIn’s largest community for AI-first remote leaders who want to stay on the pulse but don't have time for a snoozefest. Every 2 weeks we bring you the latest global news, jobs, trends & insights on the future of work. Subscribe to become a better AI-forward remote leader 👇
